rows.notes: oi-call-ref-target-wrapper is implemented-smoke and forwarded by the sweep runner: it appends a private no-op target and prepends direct ref.func/call_ref, block-returned ref.func/call_ref, typed-select target/call_ref, and dead call_ref shells to existing defined functions. A default GenValid one-case compare produced one raw mismatch with no validation/generator/command failures; the earlier pass-oi-call-ref seed-0x5eed validity blocker from stringref/exn ref pools is fixed and the profile-backed smoke now reaches comparison. The profile-backed representative first exposed a large size-losing OI-H parity gap where Binaryen directized synthetic ref.func call_ref wrappers while Starshine left call_ref/ref.func spelling. The raw skip path now directizes adjacent ref.func/block-ref.func, constant-select ref.func, and single-use local.set(ref.func)/local.get call_ref/return_call_ref wrappers, and it trims guaranteed null-as-non-null, dropped-unreachable-block, dropped null ref.test/ref.cast, and dropped non-null ref.cast trap tails before returning a skipped function, without widening the full HOT raw gate. The latest one-case target-wrapper smoke still mismatches, but OI-H call target spelling is no longer the sampled residual: Starshine and Binaryen both have 0 call_ref/table.get residuals, 20 direct `call $5` occurrences, 0 ref.test/ref.cast/br_on_cast/br_on_cast_fail, and five `unreachable` roots. The remaining 18-byte raw size gap is local-section/ref.func-drop output shape drift: Starshine drops the inserted `ref.func` uses while Binaryen keeps five dropped `ref.func` instructions. A follow-up count-3 grouped OI-H sweep at `.tmp/oi-h-target-wrapper-grouped-sweep-20260627` mixed the target-wrapper, local-carried, and effectful-sibling transforms; all three cases still mismatched, but every sampled Starshine and Binaryen output had call_ref=0, return_call_ref=0, and table.get=0. The target-wrapper case remains the local-section/ref.func-drop shape drift; the local-carried case is local-section/dropped-null shape drift after successful call_ref cleanup; the effectful-sibling case is cross-family effectful private-global if/scalar spelling drift after successful call_ref cleanup. Future OI-H work should run broader table/local/return_call_ref/multivalue sweeps to find true remaining call_ref directization gaps instead of over-mining these cross-family residuals.
